content.problemStatement:
Schools of Chefland use the 50−5050-5050−50 rule to grade their students. As per the rule , students are awarded :


`Z` grade, if their attendance is strictly less than 50%50\%50% .
`F` grade, if they score strictly less than 50%50\%50% marks, given that their attendance is greater than or equal to 50%50\%50% .
`A` grade, otherwise.
You are given two integers, XXX and YYY, denoting the percentage of attendance and percentage of marks obtained by Alice. What grade will Alice get?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the percentage of attendance and percentage of marks obtained by Alice.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the grade awarded to Alice.


Note that you may print the grade in lowercase or uppercase.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Alice's attendance is less than 50%50\%50% so she will be awarded Z grade.
Test case 333: Alice's attendance is equal to 50%50\%50% and her marks are less than 50%50\%50%, so she will be awarded F grade.
Test case 444: Alice's attendance is equal to 50%50\%50% and her marks are also equal to 50%50\%50%, so she will be awarded A grade.
